result.status_detail: MAJOR PROGRESS: 2 events parsed (IN_PROGRESS+CHAT_COMPLETE) but ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED 200 still blocks UI render
result.rendered_text_in_chat_area: (no new bubble -- only original roleplay text from prior turn visible: '차 문을 열고 내리는 순간, 습기 머금은 바람이...' user PII prompt and AI reply both absent)
result.endpoint_response_size_change_vs_605_606: same 1.1 kB, but events 0 -> 2 (parser now reads partial body before connection close)
result.frontend_typeerror_observed: net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED 200 (OK) at POST .../messages/stream (113b2qcua4vkz.js:10) -- fetch rejects despite 200 status because connection closed before clean END_STREAM completion
result.comparison_to_606: MAJOR PROGRESS at SSE layer: body now emits IN_PROGRESS+CHAT_COMPLETE events (was 0 events in 605/606). h2_end_stream=2 fix DEFINITIVELY worked at framing/body-emission layer. BUT new blocker: fetch still errors with ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED -> frontend stream handler treats response as failure -> no chat bubble rendered. The H2 stream still terminates abnormally after envelope body (not via clean END_STREAM flag on the final DATA frame).
result.h2_end_stream_fix_assessment: PARTIAL FIX CONFIRMED. h2_end_stream=2 successfully changed APF behavior from 'END_STREAM on HEADERS = no body emit' (which gave 0 parsed events in #605/#606) to 'body emitted with 2 SSE events parseable by browser'. However, the H2 stream close sequence is still wrong -- likely sending RST_STREAM or premature GOAWAY after the body instead of END_STREAM flag on the final DATA frame.
result.next_recommendation: Investigate H2 stream close in etap.log for #607. Check: (1) was END_STREAM flag set on the final DATA frame containing CHAT_COMPLETE event, OR was a separate empty DATA+END_STREAM frame sent? (2) Did APF send RST_STREAM (with what error code?) instead of natural close? (3) Did GOAWAY fire? Compare H2 close sequence with mistral/gamma successful flows. Most likely fix: ensure END_STREAM flag is on the final DATA frame (not on a subsequent empty frame, not via RST). Could be a final-flush bug in APF emitter where last DATA frame is missing the flag.

screenshots_inventory.description: DevTools EventStream tab showing 2 PARSED events: IN_PROGRESS chunkMessage + CHAT_COMPLETE replyMessage. PROGRESS vs #605/#606 (0 events).
Chat area showing only prior roleplay turn -- no user PII bubble, no AI reply, no warning rendered (fetch rejected by ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED before frontend could render)
Console error: POST .../messages/stream net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED 200 (OK) -- the only error this run (down from 5 errors in #606)

result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Cycle 94 R-27 zeta h2_end_stream=2 fix verification: MAJOR PROGRESS, NEW BLOCKER. The h2_end_stream=2 change DEFINITIVELY worked at the framing/body-emission layer -- DevTools EventStream parser now reads 2 events (IN_PROGRESS + CHAT_COMPLETE) for the first time since #605, vs 0 events in #605 and #606. This proves: (a) the SSE wire format diagnosis from #606 was correct, (b) the END_STREAM-on-HEADERS bug was the root cause of the 0-events parse, (c) 'h2_end_stream=2' setting in ai_prompt_services achieves its intended effect. HOWEVER the fix is incomplete: fetch() in the frontend still rejects with net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED (despite HTTP 200 status), so the React stream handler treats it as failure and never renders the chat bubble. The H2 stream is closing abnormally AFTER the body emits. Likely root cause: (1) the END_STREAM flag is missing on the final DATA frame containing CHAT_COMPLETE -- maybe APF sends an empty DATA frame after with END_STREAM, but the prior CHAT_COMPLETE frame did not have the flag, causing browser to perceive abnormal close, OR (2) APF sends RST_STREAM with an error code after the final DATA, triggering ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED. Recommend dev: dump etap.log H2 frame trace for #607 stream and verify the END_STREAM flag location + check for RST_STREAM/GOAWAY frames. Compare against mistral/gamma successful flows. Console errors dropped from 5 (in #606) to 1 -- showing the schema TypeError is also gone, leaving just the ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED. Next iteration likely needs only a single H2-close fix, not envelope/schema work.
obstacles_encountered: Initial 확인 click on leftover popup navigated back to home page (not chat room) -- recovered via browser back
App promotion popup ('앱에서 대화하고 더 빠르게 답변 받으세요') appeared after send -- dismissed via 확인했어요
Response tab shows 'Nothing to preview' for failed XHR -- used EventStream tab as primary evidence

notes: Cycle 94 R-27 zeta h2_end_stream=2 verification: PARTIAL with MAJOR PROGRESS + NEW BLOCKER. h2_end_stream=2 fix DEFINITIVELY worked at framing layer -- DevTools EventStream parser reads 2 events (IN_PROGRESS+CHAT_COMPLETE) for first time since #605 (was 0 events in #605/#606). #606 wire-format diagnosis CONFIRMED. END_STREAM-on-HEADERS was the original root cause. BUT new blocker: fetch rejects with net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED 200 (OK) despite events parsing. H2 stream closes abnormally AFTER body emit. Likely missing END_STREAM flag on final DATA frame OR APF sends RST_STREAM after body. Console errors 5 -> 1 (TypeError gone). Next: dump etap.log H2 frame trace for #607, verify END_STREAM flag location + check RST_STREAM/GOAWAY, compare vs mistral/gamma successful flows. Likely only a single H2-close fix needed (not envelope work). 3 PNGs in files/607/. Processed via Subagent Dispatch (4m30s, 43 tool uses).
